title: senecio wootonii
intro: Senecio wootonii, also known as Wooton's groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Senecio wootonii has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including treating fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and has also been used as a wound healer and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Senecio wootonii typically involve making tea from the leaves or roots, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected area.
